title: Some Useful Signal Models
summary: This page discusses approximations to the unit impulse function, showing that as a parameter approaches infinity the pulse height grows while width shrinks but area remains unity. It then covers multiplication of a function by an impulse, yielding φ(t)δ(t−T) = φ(T)δ(t−T), and introduces the sampling (sifting) property of the unit impulse, which states that integrating the product of a continuous function with a shifted impulse returns the function's value at the impulse location.

figures.caption: Other possible approximations to a unit impulse, showing three limiting forms: (a) a one-sided exponential αe^(−αt) as α→∞, (b) a triangular pulse of height 1/ε and base 2ε as ε→0, and (c) a Gaussian pulse of unit area as ε→0.
